headline: Malay Dignity Congress Resolutions Adopted as Government Policy Within 48 Hours
context: 4 resolutions from the 2019 Malay Dignity Congress — including strengthening Bumiputera economic participation — were adopted as government policy within 48 hours, bypassing normal policy review.

cards.big: The 2019 Malay Dignity Congress passed 8 resolutions.
cards.sub: 4 became government policy within 48 hours. No comparable non-Malay interest group has achieved this speed.

cards.big: Normal policy adoption requires Cabinet paper, inter-ministry review, and AG vetting.
cards.sub: The 48-hour adoption bypassed all standard processes.

cards.big: Resolution 3 called for Bumiputera equity targets in all GLCs.
cards.sub: This was implemented without consulting the GLC board governance framework.

cards.big: The Congress was attended by 4 university vice-chancellors in official capacity.
cards.sub: Public university involvement in an ethnically exclusive political event raises institutional neutrality questions.

cards.big: 48 hours from ethnic congress to government policy. That speed reveals where power actually lies.
Policy should serve all Malaysians through deliberative processes. When ethnic resolutions become policy faster than Cabinet papers, the system has substituted advocacy for governance.
